widgets_values:
VFX OUTPUT — AtlasCamera + ComfyUI-OCIO

Requires the ComfyUI-OCIO custom node pack:
  cd ComfyUI/custom_nodes && git clone https://github.com/SlavaSexton/ComfyUI-OCIO
  pip install opencolorio  (opencv-python-headless/tifffile/Pillow/numpy usually
  already present from other packs)
  set OPENCV_IO_ENABLE_OPENEXR=1  before launching ComfyUI (needed for EXR)

Why two export branches: AtlasExportReliefMesh bakes the SAME source image
as the mesh's 8-bit sRGB albedo texture (correct convention for DCC viewport
/ game-engine display — most OBJ/GLB viewers expect sRGB-encoded albedo, not
linear). OCIOWrite takes the same source image and converts it from ComfyUI's
native working space ('sRGB - Display' — ComfyUI holds all images this way,
see the OCIO pack's own README) into ACEScg (scene-linear), writing a proper
color-managed EXR plate — the deliverable a comp/lighting artist actually
wants, distinct from the 3D department's textured mesh.

Verified 2026-07-04: EXR magic number confirmed (762f3101), file size
consistent with a 16-bit-float zip-compressed EXR at this resolution.

PATH GOTCHA (found during testing): AtlasCamera's export nodes
(AtlasExportReliefMesh, etc.) resolve output_dir relative to ComfyUI's ROOT
working directory. OCIOWrite resolves output_folder relative to ComfyUI's
OUTPUT directory instead. Same relative path string ('atlas_exports/vfx_
ocio_demo') on both nodes therefore lands in TWO different places on disk —
ComfyUI/atlas_exports/vfx_ocio_demo/ for the mesh, ComfyUI/output/atlas_
exports/vfx_ocio_demo/ for the EXR. Use absolute paths on one or both if you
need everything co-located.

For a full Nuke/Maya pipeline, wire OCIOColorSpace (sRGB - Display -> ACEScg)
ahead of any node that needs a linear IMAGE tensor for further ComfyUI-side
compositing, rather than only writing to disk via OCIOWrite.

groups.title: ① Source + Camera Recovery + Derive Geometry
③ Comp / lighting dept — color-managed ACEScg EXR plate
